event: no history of drug addiction or intoxication
an initial provisional diagnosis of acute febrile illness with undifferentiated fever was kept
confirmed the diagnosis of scrub typhus
blood and other body fluid cultures sterile
viral markers negative for hepatitis B antigen
viral markers negative for hepatitis C antibody
ultrasound abdomen showed mild fatty infiltration of the liver
the patient was being managed conservatively
treated with tablet doxycycline 100 mg twice daily
weakness progressed to the upper limbs (Grade 2/5)
breathing difficulty with signs of respiratory distress
shifted to the Intensive Care Unit
intubated and put on mechanical ventilation
magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the brain was done which was without any abnormality
MRI of the cervical spine revealed no abnormality
nerve conduction velocity (NCV) showed motor sensory demyelinating polyneuropathy
cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) analysis did not reveal albuminocytologic dissociation
started on intravenous immunoglobulin therapy 0.4 mg/kg/day for 5 days
tablet rifampicin 600 mg twice daily for meningoencephalitis was added
gradually weaned off and extubated from the ventilator
discharged from the hospital with full neurological recovery
followed up regularly in the outpatient department
